title: Micro-SaaS Market Scan — Automated Changelog Generation for Dev Teams

findings.finding: The developer tools market exceeds $30B. Changelog and release management is a niche within this, but high-frequency touchpoint — every deploy triggers the need.
Direct competitors: Changelogfy ($29/mo), Headway (freemium), Release Notes.io. None offer deep AI comprehension of commits — most are template-based or require manual input.
Existing tools charge $15-50/mo for teams. Developer willingness to pay correlates with time saved — if it saves 2+ hours/week, $12/mo is an easy sell.
Most teams either skip changelogs entirely or assign them to junior devs as busywork. A zero-config CI/CD integration removes the friction that causes both problems.

recommendations: Launch with GitHub Actions integration only — covers 80% of target market
Free tier for public repos drives adoption and word-of-mouth
Target Product Hunt and Hacker News for initial traction
Build in public on X/Twitter to attract developer audience
